levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module gives the hybrid operating mode description for the Hybrid Electric Architecture Overview applicable to the AMPEL360E Wide Tube-and-Wing Family baseline.

The module describes the top-level operating modes, transition logic, limitation principles, degraded-mode behaviour, safe-state philosophy, crew indication context, and maintenance reporting context associated with ATLAS 070-000 / 00A.

levelledPara.title: Operating Mode Scope
levelledPara.para: The operating mode scope includes the controlled states used by the hybrid-electric architecture to manage energy availability, electrical distribution, propulsion drive operation, thermal capacity, protection functions, monitoring, maintenance reporting, and safe-state response.

This module is descriptive only. It does not define final flight crew procedures, dispatch policy, minimum equipment list logic, certified mode transition thresholds, or detailed control laws. Those items shall be controlled by the applicable operations, certification, safety, control, and maintenance baselines.

levelledPara.title: Principal Operating Modes
levelledPara.para: The hybrid-electric architecture uses the following principal operating modes:

levelledPara.title: Off Mode
levelledPara.para: Off mode is the condition in which the hybrid-electric propulsion architecture is not available for propulsion-energy transfer. Main propulsion energy paths are isolated or de-energized in accordance with the approved configuration.
Residual energy, stored energy, control power, and maintenance access conditions shall be managed in accordance with the applicable safety, servicing, and maintenance procedures.

levelledPara.title: Standby Mode
levelledPara.para: Standby mode is the condition in which selected control, monitoring, diagnostic, or support functions are available, but propulsion-energy flow is not commanded through the main propulsion path.
Standby mode supports readiness monitoring, configuration checks, pre-operational status reporting, and controlled transition to initialization mode or safe-state mode.

levelledPara.title: Initialization Mode
levelledPara.para: Initialization mode prepares the hybrid-electric architecture for normal operation. The system verifies configuration status, equipment availability, isolation state, thermal capacity, protection status, control communication, and monitoring function availability.

Initialization shall complete only when the applicable readiness criteria are satisfied. Failed readiness checks shall generate the applicable indication, maintenance message, diagnostic record, or inhibition condition.

levelledPara.title: Normal Mode
levelledPara.para: Normal mode is the operating condition in which energy source, conversion, electrical distribution, propulsion drive, thermal management, control, and monitoring functions operate within nominal architecture limits.
In normal mode, commanded propulsion demand is accepted and processed within approved equipment limits, thermal limits, protection limits, and aircraft-level operating constraints.

levelledPara.title: Monitored Mode
levelledPara.para: Monitored mode is the condition in which the architecture remains available but one or more parameters, equipment states, or diagnostic trends require increased supervision.

Monitored mode may be entered after detection of a minor abnormal condition, reduced margin, intermittent status change, or maintenance trend. The mode does not necessarily reduce available propulsion function, but it shall increase reporting or surveillance as defined by the approved baseline.

levelledPara.title: Limited Mode
levelledPara.para: Limited mode is the condition in which available propulsion energy, power, torque, thermal capacity, or system function is intentionally restricted to maintain operation within safe and controlled limits.

Limited mode may be caused by reduced energy availability, reduced thermal capacity, protection coordination, degraded equipment status, or command authority limitation. The limitation shall be reported to the applicable indication, recording, maintenance, and diagnostic functions.

levelledPara.title: Degraded Mode
levelledPara.para: Degraded mode is the condition in which the hybrid-electric architecture continues controlled operation after partial loss of function, equipment degradation, monitoring degradation, or detected abnormal condition.

Degraded mode shall prevent uncontrolled fault propagation across energy source, electrical distribution, propulsion drive, thermal, control, and monitoring boundaries. Degraded operation may require reduced function, load shedding, increased reporting, dispatch limitation, or transition to safe-state mode.

levelledPara.title: Ground Service Mode
levelledPara.para: Ground service mode supports ground-based servicing, inspection, configuration checks, energy path preparation, controlled diagnostic activities, and support equipment interaction when permitted by the approved aircraft configuration.
Ground service mode shall preserve electrical safety, energy isolation, protection status, data integrity, configuration control, and maintenance traceability.

levelledPara.title: Maintenance Mode
levelledPara.para: Maintenance mode supports controlled access to diagnostic information, built-in-test functions, configuration data, maintenance messages, equipment status, and ground support interfaces.

Maintenance mode shall inhibit unsafe propulsion-energy transfer unless specifically authorized by an approved test or maintenance procedure. Maintenance access shall remain configuration-controlled and traceable.

levelledPara.title: Safe-State Mode
levelledPara.para: Safe-state mode is the condition in which the architecture commands or maintains a controlled state intended to prevent unsafe energy transfer, uncontrolled propulsion output, thermal exceedance, electrical fault propagation, or uncontrolled system behaviour.

Safe-state mode may include energy isolation, controlled shutdown, load removal, protection trip retention, degraded monitoring, maintenance message generation, and locked inhibition of further operation until approved recovery conditions are satisfied.

levelledPara.title: Mode Transition Philosophy
levelledPara.para: Mode transitions shall be deterministic, traceable, and controlled by validated transition criteria. A transition shall occur only when the applicable command validity, equipment status, protection status, thermal status, energy availability, and monitoring criteria are satisfied.

Invalid, incomplete, conflicting, or unsafe transition conditions shall be rejected, inhibited, or redirected to a controlled limited, degraded, or safe-state condition as defined by the approved system baseline.

levelledPara.title: Mode Transition Inputs
levelledPara.para: Operating mode transition inputs may include aircraft-level command requests, propulsion demand, source availability, conversion status, electrical protection status, thermal capacity, equipment health, sensor validity, communication status, maintenance status, and detected abnormal conditions.

levelledPara.title: Mode Transition Outputs
levelledPara.para: Operating mode transition outputs may include propulsion availability status, energy path enablement, contactor or isolation state, permitted power level, limitation state, crew indication, maintenance message, diagnostic record, protection status, and ground support access status.

levelledPara.title: Protection Effects on Operating Mode
levelledPara.para: Protection functions may command or inhibit operating mode changes when abnormal electrical, thermal, propulsion-drive, control, monitoring, or communication conditions are detected.

Protection effects may include load limitation, energy path isolation, drive inhibition, thermal limitation, controlled shutdown, safe-state transition, maintenance reporting, and retention of fault data for subsequent investigation.

levelledPara.title: Thermal Effects on Operating Mode
levelledPara.para: Reduced thermal capacity or thermal exceedance may cause transition from normal mode to monitored, limited, degraded, or safe-state mode. Thermal status shall be considered when allocating propulsion power, permitting continued operation, or authorizing recovery.

levelledPara.title: Energy Availability Effects on Operating Mode
levelledPara.para: Reduced energy source availability, conversion limitation, buffering limitation, or electrical distribution limitation may restrict the operating mode available to the architecture.
The system shall report energy availability status and shall inhibit operating modes that require energy levels, conversion capability, or distribution capacity that are not available.

levelledPara.title: Crew Indication Context
levelledPara.para: Operating mode status, limitation status, degraded status, and safe-state status shall be made available to the applicable crew indication and recording functions as required by the approved aircraft configuration.

Detailed alerting logic, message text, crew action logic, and operational procedures are outside the scope of this data module and shall be controlled by the applicable operations and certification baseline.

levelledPara.title: Maintenance Reporting Context
levelledPara.para: Operating mode changes, inhibited transitions, protection events, degraded function, safe-state entry, and failed readiness checks shall generate maintenance or diagnostic records as required by the approved maintenance reporting baseline.
Corrective action references, fault isolation logic, dispatch effects, and return-to-service tests shall be defined in the applicable fault isolation and maintenance data modules.

levelledPara.title: Recovery Philosophy
levelledPara.para: Recovery from limited, degraded, maintenance, or safe-state mode shall occur only when approved reset, inspection, readiness, or maintenance criteria are satisfied.

Automatic recovery, crew-commanded recovery, maintenance-commanded recovery, and ground-support recovery shall be separately controlled by the approved system safety, control, operations, and maintenance baselines.

levelledPara.title: Operating Mode Boundaries
levelledPara.para: This data module defines operating modes at architecture level only. It does not allocate certified thresholds, final annunciation text, failure probabilities, time limits, dispatch categories, control-law parameters, or maintenance task intervals.
Those values shall be defined in controlled engineering datasets, safety assessment records, operational documentation, maintenance procedures, verification plans, and certification evidence.

levelledPara.title: Traceability Context
levelledPara.para: This data module supports deterministic traceability between architecture-level operating mode definition, control logic, protection logic, S1000D CSDB descriptive content, maintenance reporting, safety assessment evidence, verification evidence, and lifecycle governance records.

levelledPara.title: Authoring Status
levelledPara.para: This issue is an unverified scaffold. The content shall be completed, reviewed, and validated against the applicable BREX data module, business rules, applicability model, terminology rules, operating mode baseline, control baseline, interface control baseline, programme configuration baseline, safety assessment evidence, and verification records before CSDB release.
